__note: MASTER copy for the drug-assistance system (shared module, ADR-002 physical split): ONLY human-facing copy lives here, joined to the operational data in src/data/drugAssistance.ts by id/key/slug. URLs, phones, program names, statuses NEVER appear in this file — the quarterly audit edits the TS module and every locale follows. Field mask in ../masks.json.

shell.title: {drug} Assistance Programs — Help Paying for {drug} | AltaMedicare
shell.description: How to get help paying for {drug} ({generic}) on Medicare: manufacturer patient assistance, nonprofit copay grants, and Extra Help. Why copay coupons don't work on Medicare — and what does.
shell.lede: Help paying for {drug} ({generic}) — manufacturer patient assistance, nonprofit copay grants, and Medicare Extra Help for {conditionLabel}.
shell.calloutHtml: On Medicare? You generally cannot use a {drug} copay coupon or savings card — federal anti-kickback rules block them. But the manufacturer's Patient Assistance Program (free drug by income) and nonprofit foundation grants still can help. Below are the routes that work with Medicare.
shell.secManufacturerBlurb: The company that makes {drug} may provide it free or at low cost to people who qualify by income — including many Medicare beneficiaries.
shell.secFoundationBlurb: Disease-based charities that give grants you can use with Medicare for {conditionLabel}. Funds open and close based on availability.
shell.secGovernmentBlurb: Federal and state programs that lower your overall drug and Medicare costs.
shell.secDatabaseBlurb: Free databases that list additional programs and discounts.
shell.finderCalloutHtml: Not sure which one fits you? Use the free Prescription Drug Assistance Finder and its quick eligibility estimator, read Does Medicare cover {drug}?, or let Bret screen you and help you apply.

faqs.q: Can I use a {drug} copay card if I have Medicare?
faqs.a: Usually no. Federal anti-kickback rules generally prohibit people with Medicare from using manufacturer copay coupons or savings cards for {drug}. But you may still qualify for the manufacturer's Patient Assistance Program, which provides {drug} free or at low cost based on income.

faqs.q: How do I get help paying for {drug}?
faqs.a: Start with the {drug} manufacturer assistance program, check whether a nonprofit foundation has an open fund for your condition, and apply for Medicare Extra Help if your income is limited. A licensed agent can screen you and help you apply — free.

faqs.q: Is {drug} covered by Medicare Part D?
faqs.a: {drug} is often on Part D and Medicare Advantage drug formularies, frequently on a higher brand or specialty tier and sometimes with prior authorization. Coverage and cost vary by plan. See our guide on whether Medicare covers {drug}, or have us check your specific plan.
Yes. Every program here is free to apply for. Never pay a fee to enroll in Extra Help, a foundation grant, or a manufacturer assistance program — that's a red flag for a scam.

shell.finderBtn: Open the full Drug Assistance Finder
shell.ctaText: Send Bret your medication list — he'll check assistance programs, Extra Help, and which Part D plan covers {drug} for the least. Free, no pressure.
shell.smsBody: Hi Bret, can you help me afford {drug}?

programs.tagline: Federal subsidy that lowers or eliminates Part D premiums, deductibles, and copays.
programs.helps: Lowers or eliminates your Part D premium, deductible, and drug copays. Many people who qualify never apply.
programs.eligibility: Based on limited income and resources. Apply free through Social Security; if you have Medicaid, SSI, or a Medicare Savings Program you may qualify automatically.

programs.tagline: State programs that pay Medicare costs and can unlock Extra Help automatically.
programs.helps: Can pay your Part B premium and, by qualifying you, automatically grant Extra Help for drug costs.
programs.typical: May pay your Part B premium and unlock Extra Help for drug costs.
programs.eligibility: Income- and resource-based; administered by your state (Utah Department of Workforce Services / Medicaid). Limits are higher than many people expect.

programs.tagline: Grants for out-of-pocket drug costs across 70+ disease funds.
programs.helps: Provides grants that cover copays, coinsurance, and other out-of-pocket medication costs. Can be used with Medicare.
programs.eligibility: Diagnosis must match an open disease fund; income generally up to 400–500% of the federal poverty level. Funds open and close based on availability.

programs.tagline: Grants for copays, premiums, and even travel costs by disease fund.
programs.helps: Covers copays, coinsurance, premiums, deductibles, and sometimes travel costs for a covered condition.
programs.typical: May help with copays, premiums, deductibles, and sometimes travel costs.
programs.eligibility: Diagnosis must match an open fund; income generally up to 400–500% of the federal poverty level. Medicare beneficiaries are eligible.

programs.tagline: Copay and travel assistance for costly chronic and life-threatening conditions.
programs.helps: Helps cover copays and treatment-related travel for high-cost medications and chronic diseases.
programs.typical: May help with copays and treatment-related travel.
programs.eligibility: Diagnosis-based with income limits; funds depend on availability. Open to people with Medicare.

programs.tagline: Copay grants plus free case management for many diseases.
programs.helps: Provides copay grants and connects you with a case manager who can help navigate insurance and appeals.
programs.eligibility: Diagnosis must match an open fund; income generally up to 400% of the federal poverty level. Available to Medicare beneficiaries.

programs.tagline: Free searchable database of assistance and discount programs.
programs.helps: A free directory of patient assistance programs, manufacturer offers, diagnosis-based aid, and a drug discount card.
programs.eligibility: Free to use for anyone; individual programs in the database set their own rules.

programs.tagline: PhRMA's search tool for hundreds of manufacturer programs.
programs.helps: Searches across hundreds of biopharmaceutical patient assistance programs to find ones for your medications.
programs.eligibility: Free to use; results depend on each program's criteria.

programs.tagline: Directory of manufacturer patient assistance programs.
programs.helps: A free, regularly updated list of company programs that provide free or low-cost medicines.
programs.eligibility: Free to use; each listed program sets its own eligibility.

programs.tagline: Free Bristol Myers Squibb medicines for eligible patients (Eliquis, Revlimid, and more).
programs.helps: Provides eligible Bristol Myers Squibb medications free of charge. Eliquis is also supported through Eliquis 360 Support.
programs.eligibility: Generally for uninsured or underinsured patients meeting income limits; Medicare beneficiaries may qualify through the foundation (not the copay card).

programs.tagline: Free Janssen medicines for eligible patients (Xarelto, Stelara, Tremfya, Imbruvica).
programs.helps: Provides eligible Johnson & Johnson / Janssen prescription products at no cost. Janssen CarePath offers added support.
programs.eligibility: Income-based; generally for uninsured/underinsured patients. Medicare patients may qualify through the foundation.

programs.tagline: Patient assistance for Ozempic, insulins, and other Novo Nordisk drugs.
programs.helps: The Novo Nordisk Patient Assistance Program provides free medication to those who qualify; NovoCare helps you navigate coverage.
programs.eligibility: Generally for U.S. residents who are uninsured or have Medicare and meet income limits (up to ~400% FPL for the PAP).

programs.tagline: Free Eli Lilly medicines for eligible patients (Mounjaro, Trulicity, insulins).
programs.helps: Provides eligible Lilly medications free of charge to qualifying patients.
programs.eligibility: Income-based; available to many Medicare Part D patients who meet criteria. Lilly also offers an Insulin Value Program ($35/month).

programs.tagline: Free Boehringer Ingelheim medicines (Jardiance, Ofev, Spiriva).
programs.helps: Provides eligible Boehringer Ingelheim medications at no cost through the BI Cares Foundation.
programs.eligibility: Income-based; open to qualifying uninsured and Medicare patients.

programs.tagline: Free AstraZeneca medicines (Farxiga, Brilinta, Symbicort, oncology).
programs.helps: Provides eligible AstraZeneca medications at no cost, with a dedicated track for Medicare Part D patients.
programs.eligibility: Income-based; AZ&Me has a specific Medicare Part D eligibility pathway.

programs.tagline: Free Novartis medicines (Entresto, Cosentyx, Leqvio).
programs.helps: Provides eligible Novartis medications free of charge to qualifying patients.
programs.eligibility: Income-based; available to uninsured and Medicare patients who meet criteria.

programs.tagline: Free AbbVie medicines for eligible patients (Humira, Skyrizi, Rinvoq).
programs.helps: Provides eligible AbbVie medications at no cost to qualifying patients.
programs.eligibility: Income-based; serves uninsured and many Medicare patients who meet criteria.

programs.tagline: Free Amgen medicines for eligible patients (Enbrel, Repatha, Prolia).
programs.helps: Provides eligible Amgen medications free of charge through the Safety Net Foundation.
programs.eligibility: Income-based; includes a pathway for Medicare patients who meet criteria.

programs.tagline: Free Sanofi/Regeneron medicines (Dupixent, Praluent, Lantus).
programs.helps: Connects eligible patients to free medication and support; Dupixent MyWay offers additional help.
programs.eligibility: Income-based; serves uninsured and qualifying Medicare patients.

programs.tagline: Help accessing many Pfizer medicines (Ibrance, Xeljanz, Eliquis).
programs.helps: Connects eligible patients with assistance programs that provide Pfizer medicines free or at a savings.
programs.eligibility: Income-based; includes options for insured and uninsured patients (free-drug track for those who qualify).

programs.tagline: Patient assistance for Astellas medicines (Xtandi, Myrbetriq).
programs.helps: Provides eligible Astellas medications free or low-cost and helps with coverage navigation.
programs.eligibility: Income-based; includes support for Medicare patients who meet criteria.

programs.tagline: Free GSK medicines for eligible patients (Trelegy, Breo, Anoro).
programs.helps: Provides eligible GSK medications at no cost through the GSK for You / Bridges to Access programs.
programs.eligibility: Income-based; serves uninsured and qualifying Medicare patients.
